citation: Yip, Virginia and Stephen Matthews. 2000. Syntactic transfer in a Cantonese-English bilingual child. Bilingualism: Language and Cognition 3(3):193–208.
claim_supported: Cantonese finite clauses permit null subjects and objects; specific null objects are discourse-recoverable; overt topicalization provides one related antecedence path.
limitations: Study centers bilingual acquisition and a null-topic analysis; it does not establish one universal derivation or full subject distribution.

citation: Lee, Patrick Chi-Wai. 2018. A Study of Anaphoric Non-specific Object Drop in Cantonese: Classification and Derivation. Current Research in Chinese Linguistics 97(1):245–257.
claim_supported: Specific and non-specific object drop; existential, generic, and attributive subtypes; ambiguity and predicate effects; examples checked with 20 Hong Kong-born speakers.
limitations: NP ellipsis with null D is theory-specific; small judgment check and constructed examples do not establish all verbs, contexts, or regions.

citation: Matthews, Stephen and Virginia Yip. 2011. Cantonese: A Comprehensive Grammar, 2nd ed.
claim_supported: Omitted-pronoun subject interpreted from the discourse situation.
limitations: Single teaching example does not establish a full null-subject matrix.

citation: Holmberg, Anders. 2007. Null subjects and polarity focus. Studia Linguistica 61(3):212–236.
claim_supported: Cantonese permits null subjects but requires an overt coreferential subject in the tested polarity-focus second conjunct.
limitations: Specific theory and contexts do not supply a general licensing algorithm.

citation: Lee, Tommy Tsz-Ming and Victor Junnan Pan. 2024. Licensing VP Movement and Ellipsis in Mandarin and Cantonese. Proceedings of WCCFL 40:192–201.
claim_supported: Selective VP-ellipsis licensing by particular modal, auxiliary, and aspectual heads; progressive and other heads provide negative controls.
limitations: Theory-specific VP analysis does not determine null-NP arguments, fragments, or every lexical use.

citation: Huang and Yang. 2024. Study distinguishing Chinese-type topic drop from other pro-drop phenomena.
claim_supported: Distinguishes null topic, pro drop, CP-edge locality, and root-clause effects as separate analytical questions.
limitations: Not a Cantonese distribution study; use only to frame boundaries.

citation: Canto Span v0.5.208 runtime audit for UC-RQ-025.
claim_supported: Narrow overt-topic chain, unresolved missing-slot signals, cross-sentence gaps, and broad intransitive/full-coverage collisions.
limitations: Runtime behavior has zero linguistic evidence weight and must be rerun on the current branch before coding.
